title: Chains — Create / Edit / Delete
deliverable: Full in-memory chain management: a ChainEdit form component (create + edit), delete with Turkish confirmation dialog, 'Yeni zincir' entry point from the Chains list, and extended useChains hook with createChain / updateChain / deleteChain. Default (is_default) chains are editable but protected from deletion — a 'Sıfırla' reset path restores the original seed values instead. Position ordering is assigned on create. All mutations operate on the in-memory chains collection provided by Block 12; no persistence yet.

in: components/ChainEdit.jsx — create/edit form reachable via view state 'chain-edit'
Create flow: name field, single-emoji field (with validation: exactly one grapheme cluster), steps textarea (one step per line)
'Yeni zincir' button in Chains list view that transitions to 'chain-edit' with no pre-filled chain (create mode)
Edit flow: same form pre-filled with the selected chain's data; triggered by an edit affordance on each chain row in Chains list
Delete action on each chain row in Chains list with a Turkish inline confirmation ('Silmek istediğine emin misin?')
is_default === true chains: editable (name, emoji, steps may change) but NOT deletable — delete button replaced with 'Sıfırla' which restores seed values
hooks/useChains.js extended with createChain(name, emoji, steps[]) / updateChain(id, fields) / deleteChain(id) / resetChain(id)
Position assignment on create: new chain appended at position = max(existing positions) + 1
Form validation: name must be non-empty, emoji must be non-empty, steps must contain at least one non-blank line
Turkish UI copy throughout (field labels, placeholders, button labels, error messages, confirmation text)
Back navigation from ChainEdit to 'chains' view on save or cancel
Emerald/gold inline styles consistent with project design tokens
out: Loading a chain into the queue (Block 12)
Reordering steps within the ChainEdit form (beyond textarea order)
Image/icon upload — emoji picker widget (plain text input only)
Auth or user-id association (anonymous context only in this block)

files.purpose: Create/edit form for a chain. Accepts props: chain (null for create, chain object for edit), onSave(updatedChain), onCancel(). Renders name input, emoji input, steps textarea (one per line), Save/Cancel buttons. Shows 'Sıfırla' instead of delete for is_default chains; shows delete button with inline confirmation for user chains. Validates on submit and shows inline Turkish error copy.

Add 'Yeni zincir' button that sets view to 'chain-edit' with no selected chain. Add edit pencil icon and delete/reset button to each chain row. Wire delete confirmation inline per row. Pass selected chain to App-level state so ChainEdit receives it.

Extend with createChain, updateChain, deleteChain, resetChain. createChain appends at max position + 1. deleteChain guards against is_default (no-op or throws). resetChain restores the matching DEFAULT_CHAINS seed entry by id. All mutations update in-memory state; storage write is a no-op stub (/* TODO Block 14 */) so the hook interface is stable.

Handle 'chain-edit' view state: render <ChainEdit /> when view === 'chain-edit'. Pass selectedChain state and setView/setSelectedChain props. Wire onSave to call useChains.createChain or updateChain based on whether selectedChain is null. Wire onCancel to return to 'chains' view.

implementation_steps.description: Extend useChains hook with CRUD + reset mutations
implementation_steps.details: In src/hooks/useChains.js add four functions exported from the hook. createChain(name, emoji, stepsArray): generates a client-side UUID (crypto.randomUUID()), computes position = chains.reduce((max, c) => Math.max(max, c.position), -1) + 1, constructs a new chain object { id, name, emoji, steps: stepsArray, position, is_default: false, created_at: new Date().toISOString() }, calls setChains(prev => [...prev, newChain]). updateChain(id, fields): calls setChains(prev => prev.map(c => c.id === id ? { ...c, ...fields } : c)). deleteChain(id): guards with if (chains.find(c => c.id === id)?.is_default) return; then calls setChains(prev => prev.filter(c => c.id !== id)). resetChain(id): finds the matching entry in DEFAULT_CHAINS by comparing id (default chains carry fixed hardcoded UUIDs), calls updateChain(id, { name: seed.name, emoji: seed.emoji, steps: seed.steps }). All four functions also call the storage stub /* await storage.saveChains(nextChains) — wired in Block 14 */.

Create src/components/ChainEdit.jsx. Props: { chain, onSave, onCancel }. Local state: name (string), emoji (string), stepsText (string — textarea value). On mount / when chain prop changes, initialise local state from chain fields or empty strings. Render: a back/cancel button row at top; a 'name' text input (label: 'Zincir adı', placeholder: 'Sabah ritüeli…'); an 'emoji' text input (label: 'Emoji', placeholder: '☀️', maxLength attribute set to 8 chars but validated to one grapheme cluster); a 'steps' textarea (label: 'Adımlar (her satıra bir adım)', placeholder: 'Yüzünü yıka
Su iç
…', rows=8); a 'Kaydet' submit button; for non-default chains an inline delete row (see step 13.4); for is_default chains a 'Sıfırla' button. Validation on submit: trim name — if empty show error 'Zincir adı boş olamaz'; validate emoji via [...emoji.trim()].length === 1 — if not show 'Tek bir emoji gir'; filter steps lines (split by newline, trim, filter non-empty) — if zero lines show 'En az bir adım ekle'. On valid submit call onSave({ ...chain, name: name.trim(), emoji: emoji.trim(), steps: stepsLines }) — caller decides create vs update.

In src/App.jsx add selectedChain state (null | chain object). Add 'chain-edit' to the view union. When view === 'chain-edit' render <ChainEdit chain={selectedChain} onSave={handleChainSave} onCancel={() => setView('chains')} />. handleChainSave: if selectedChain is null call createChain(data.name, data.emoji, data.steps), else call updateChain(selectedChain.id, { name, emoji, steps }); then setView('chains'). Expose setSelectedChain and setView to Chains component so it can navigate to 'chain-edit' with the correct chain or null.

implementation_steps.description: Add inline delete confirmation to ChainEdit for user chains
implementation_steps.details: Inside ChainEdit, maintain local boolean deleteConfirming (default false). For is_default === false (or is_default is absent/falsy) render a delete row below the form. When deleteConfirming === false: show a trash icon button labelled 'Sil' (muted red tint). When clicked set deleteConfirming = true; the row transforms to an inline confirmation strip: 'Silmek istediğine emin misin?' with 'Evet, sil' (destructive gold/red) and 'Vazgeç' buttons side by side. 'Evet, sil' calls deleteChain(chain.id) then onCancel() (returns to chains list). 'Vazgeç' resets deleteConfirming = false. For is_default === true render a 'Sıfırla' button instead; clicking it calls resetChain(chain.id) and shows a brief inline toast 'Varsayılana döndürüldü' for 1500ms.

implementation_steps.description: Update Chains list view with edit and delete affordances and 'Yeni zincir' button
implementation_steps.details: In src/components/Chains.jsx add a 'Yeni zincir' button at the bottom of the list (or top — designer's choice; recommend bottom to match mobile thumb reach). onClick: setSelectedChain(null); setView('chain-edit'). On each chain row add a small edit icon button (pencil, accessible label 'Düzenle'). onClick: setSelectedChain(chain); setView('chain-edit'). Remove any inline delete from the list row — delete is now handled inside ChainEdit to keep the list clean and prevent accidental taps. This keeps the list view calm and single-purpose.

Add a small utility function isOneEmoji(str) to src/lib/utils.js (create file if not present, otherwise add export). Implementation: const segs = [...new Intl.Segmenter().segment(str.trim())]; return segs.length === 1. This correctly handles multi-codepoint emoji (e.g. 🕌 = U+1F54C is one grapheme, flags like 🇹🇷 are two codepoints but one grapheme). Use this in ChainEdit validation rather than character count. Fall back gracefully if Intl.Segmenter is not available (Safari < 16): fall back to str.trim().length > 0 && str.trim().length <= 8 with a note.

implementation_steps.description: Style ChainEdit to match project design language
implementation_steps.details: Use inline styles referencing CSS vars (--color-bg, --color-surface, --color-gold, --color-emerald, --color-text-primary, --color-text-muted). Form inputs: background var(--color-surface), border 1px solid rgba(gold, 0.3), border-radius 8px, padding 10px 14px, color var(--color-text-primary), font-size 1rem. Focused inputs: border-color var(--color-gold), outline none. Textarea: same treatment, resize vertical. Kaydet button: background var(--color-emerald), color white, border none, border-radius 8px, padding 12px 24px, font-weight 600, min-height 44px. Error messages: color #ef4444 (red), font-size 0.8rem, margin-top 4px. The delete confirmation strip: background rgba(239,68,68,0.1), border-radius 8px, padding 10px. Sıfırla button: gold outline style. All tap targets >= 44px.

data_structures.name: Chain
data_structures.shape: { id: string (UUID), name: string, emoji: string, steps: string[], position: number, is_default: boolean, created_at: string (ISO8601) }
data_structures.notes: Shape is identical to Block 12's in-memory chain object and mirrors the Supabase 'chains' table schema (§4.3). The is_default field controls edit/delete behaviour: user-created chains (is_default: false) can be fully deleted; default chains (is_default: true) can be edited (name/emoji/steps mutated in memory) but cannot be deleted — resetChain restores them to DEFAULT_CHAINS seed values.

data_structures.name: DEFAULT_CHAINS
data_structures.shape: Chain[] — the 7 seed chains defined in hooks/useChains.js (or a sibling constants file). Each entry carries a fixed hardcoded UUID as the stable reset lookup key.
data_structures.notes: The reset lookup uses the fixed hardcoded UUID as the stable key. Default chain UUIDs are: Namaz Hazırlığı=b89e0940-9583-4803-9210-d73924e6cfa4, Odak Bloğu=16064f10-a06a-4945-8340-5d0277ebe288, Sabah Demiri=17d995ea-0b2f-4110-b3c5-8ff06778a4bb, Abdest=f905b229-be82-43c5-a267-6f9c38d5c038, Uyku Hazırlığı=c1ba5c76-b02b-4f5b-bd85-cfe11ab91b1f, Evi Topla=3a519d0d-f060-4f1b-be5f-efec4325610a, Güne Başlama=72990e01-d127-410e-88cd-f0c2e3b9edc0. These UUIDs are shared verbatim with Blocks 12/14/18. User-created chains use crypto.randomUUID().

data_structures.name: ChainEditLocalState
data_structures.shape: { name: string, emoji: string, stepsText: string, errors: { name?: string, emoji?: string, steps?: string }, deleteConfirming: boolean, resetToastVisible: boolean }
data_structures.notes: Entirely component-local; not exported or shared. stepsText is the raw textarea value; parsed into string[] only on submit (split by newline, trim, filter non-empty).

interfaces.name: createChain
interfaces.signature: createChain(name: string, emoji: string, steps: string[]): void
interfaces.purpose: Appends a new user chain to the in-memory chains array with a generated UUID and next available position. is_default always false.

interfaces.name: updateChain
interfaces.purpose: Merges fields into the matching chain. Works for both user and default chains (no guard here — caller must enforce is_default rules at UI level).

interfaces.name: deleteChain
interfaces.purpose: Removes a user chain from state. No-op if the chain is is_default — guards internally to prevent accidental deletion of seeded chains.

interfaces.name: resetChain
interfaces.purpose: Restores a default chain's name, emoji, and steps to the original DEFAULT_CHAINS seed values. Looks up by fixed hardcoded UUID (stable across all blocks).

interfaces.name: ChainEdit props
interfaces.signature: { chain: Chain | null, onSave: (data: { name: string, emoji: string, steps: string[] }) => void, onCancel: () => void }
interfaces.purpose: chain === null signals create mode; non-null signals edit mode. onSave receives only the mutable fields; App.jsx decides whether to call createChain or updateChain. onCancel navigates back to 'chains' view.

interfaces.name: isOneEmoji
interfaces.purpose: Validates that a string contains exactly one grapheme cluster (emoji). Uses Intl.Segmenter with a safe fallback for older Safari.

acceptance_criteria: Tapping 'Yeni zincir' in the Chains list navigates to ChainEdit in create mode with all fields blank.
Submitting ChainEdit with valid name, emoji, and at least one non-blank step adds the chain to the in-memory list and returns to the Chains list view.
The newly created chain appears in the Chains list at the bottom (highest position value).
Tapping the edit icon on any chain row navigates to ChainEdit with that chain's fields pre-filled.
Saving an edited chain updates the in-memory record; changes are reflected immediately in the Chains list.
The delete flow for a user chain shows an inline 'Silmek istediğine emin misin?' confirmation before removing.
Confirming delete removes the chain from the list; cancelling leaves it intact.
A default (is_default: true) chain shows 'Sıfırla' instead of a delete button.
Tapping 'Sıfırla' on a default chain restores its original name, emoji, and steps from the seed constant.
Attempting to submit ChainEdit with an empty name field shows the Turkish error 'Zincir adı boş olamaz'.
Attempting to submit with a multi-grapheme or empty emoji field shows 'Tek bir emoji gir'.
Attempting to submit with no non-blank step lines shows 'En az bir adım ekle'.
All form inputs and buttons meet the 44px minimum tap target height.
Pressing Cancel or Back from ChainEdit returns to the Chains list without modifying any chain.
No calls to localStorage or Supabase are made — all mutations are in-memory only in this block.
No new npm packages are installed.
verification: Open the Chains list → tap 'Yeni zincir' → form appears blank. Fill valid data → Kaydet → new chain visible at bottom of list.
Tap edit on an existing user chain → fields pre-filled → change name → Kaydet → updated name shown in list.

Tap edit on any default chain (e.g. 'Namaz Hazırlığı') → confirm no delete button, only 'Sıfırla' → change the name → Kaydet → name updated in list → tap edit again → tap Sıfırla → name restored to original.
Tap edit on a user chain → tap Sil → confirmation strip appears → tap Vazgeç → chain still present. Tap Sil again → tap 'Evet, sil' → chain removed from list.
Submit ChainEdit with empty name → error message shown, no navigation. Fix name, submit → saves.
Submit with emoji field containing 'ab' (two characters, not one emoji) → emoji error shown.
Submit with steps textarea containing only whitespace/blank lines → steps error shown.
Confirm no localStorage writes occur during create/edit/delete by observing localStorage in DevTools before and after operations.
Visually check: all interactive elements have adequate tap target size; styles match emerald/gold theme.

questionable_decisions.decision: Default chains are editable but protected from deletion; 'Sıfırla' restores seed values.
questionable_decisions.assumption: Users may want to localise step wording (e.g. rewrite 'abdest al' in their own phrasing) but should never permanently lose the curated default content. Deletion of defaults would be irreversible until Block 14 re-seeds from localStorage or Supabase.
alternatives: Allow deletion of default chains (cannot be restored without a full re-seed)
Make default chains fully read-only (no edit at all, only load)
Allow deletion but show a 'Varsayılanları geri yükle' bulk-reset option in settings
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep the current decision: editable + Sıfırla. It respects ADHD user personalisation without creating a footgun. Revisit after Block 14 when seeds can be re-applied reliably.

questionable_decisions.decision: Delete confirmation is inline within ChainEdit, not on the Chains list row.
questionable_decisions.assumption: Showing a destructive confirmation on the list row risks accidental taps in a tightly packed mobile list. Moving it inside the edit form adds one intentional navigation step before any irreversible action.
alternatives: Inline confirmation directly on the list row (faster path, higher accidental-tap risk on mobile)
Modal/dialog overlay for confirmation (more friction, breaks the 'no modals' pattern elsewhere)
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep delete inside ChainEdit. One extra tap is acceptable; the main queue view stays calm and uncluttered. If user research shows friction is a problem, move to list-row inline.

questionable_decisions.decision: Emoji field is a plain text input validated to exactly one grapheme cluster using Intl.Segmenter.
questionable_decisions.assumption: An emoji picker widget adds a dependency and cognitive overhead; the user population (primarily the author) is comfortable typing emoji directly. Intl.Segmenter handles multi-codepoint emoji correctly on modern browsers.
alternatives: Emoji picker library (e.g. emoji-mart) — richer UX, +~50KB bundle
Restrict to single Unicode codepoint (breaks flags, family emoji, ZWJ sequences)
No validation — accept any non-empty string as the emoji field
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Plain input + Intl.Segmenter with a graceful fallback is correct for this scope. Add the emoji picker only if Phase 3 or community features expand the chain-creation audience.

questionable_decisions.decision: Position is assigned as max(existing positions) + 1 on create, with no gap-filling or re-indexing.
questionable_decisions.assumption: Block 13 operates on in-memory state only. Block 14 will persist positions to localStorage as a simple sorted array index. Gaps created by deletion are acceptable because Block 14 can normalise positions on write. Re-indexing in Block 13 would couple this block to persistence concerns.
alternatives: Re-index all positions after every delete (avoids gaps but adds unnecessary complexity pre-Block 14)
Use creation timestamp as implicit ordering (simpler, but breaks once drag-to-reorder is added in Phase 2)
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep gap-tolerant position assignment. Document that Block 14 should normalise positions on save, and Phase 2 drag-to-reorder should compute a clean integer sequence.

questionable_decisions.decision: resetChain looks up the seed entry by fixed hardcoded UUID; default chain IDs are stable constants shared across all blocks.
questionable_decisions.assumption: Default chains carry fixed hardcoded UUIDs defined in DEFAULT_CHAINS, eliminating any name-lookup fragility. User-created chains use crypto.randomUUID() at creation time.
alternatives: Look up by name (fragile if names are localised or edited)
Defer Sıfırla to Block 14 when IDs are stabilised by persistence
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Use fixed hardcoded UUIDs in DEFAULT_CHAINS. This eliminates lookup ambiguity and aligns with Blocks 12/14/18.
questionable_decisions.resolution: C1 — locked by user: defaults carry fixed hardcoded UUIDs (listed), reset/lookup is by UUID; user-created chains use crypto.randomUUID().

open_questions: Should editing a default chain's steps also update any queue_items that were loaded from that chain before the edit? (Likely no — loaded steps are already flat text in the queue, not references. Confirm with Block 12 design.)
Is there a maximum number of steps per chain? The PRD's longest default chain has 7 steps. Should ChainEdit enforce a soft limit (e.g. warn at 20+) to prevent accidental queue flooding?
Should the 'Yeni zincir' button be at the top or bottom of the Chains list? Top is discoverable; bottom is more thumb-friendly on mobile. PRD does not specify.
After Block 14, should resetChain write the restored seed values to localStorage immediately, or defer to the normal save path? Needs coordination with the Block 14 storage interface.

Block 12 spec is not yet written — confirm that useChains.js in Block 12 exports the chains array and a setChains (or equivalent) mechanism that Block 13 can extend, rather than owning the state internally.
